Using `selectFrom()` on a joined table fetches all columns from all joined tables, which retrieves unnecessary data and can impact performance. Explicitly specify only the columns needed, such as `dsl.select(BUILD_META.BUILD_NAME, BUILD_META.CREATED_AT, BUILD_VERSION.CHECKSUM, BUILD_VERSION.VERSION_NUMBER, BUILD_VERSION.METADATA, BUILD_SUPPORTED_FORMATS.FORMAT_NAME).from(...)`.

Using `selectFrom()` on a joined table fetches all columns from all joined tables, retrieving more data than necessary. Explicitly select only the required columns like `BUILD_META.BUILD_NAME`, `BUILD_VERSION.CHECKSUM`, `BUILD_VERSION.VERSION_NUMBER`, `BUILD_VERSION.METADATA`, and `BUILD_SUPPORTED_FORMATS.FORMAT_NAME`.
